name: SPOT

overview.value: SPOT 6 is designed to efficiently cover huge areas in record time, delivering 1.5m resolution products suited to topographic mapping from 1:30,000 to 1:7,500 scale. It operates on the same orbit as the very high-resolution Pleiades satellites. The SPOT program builds on a legacy of data delivery that began 40 years ago with the launch of the original SPOT 1 satellite in 1986.

orbit.value: same orbit as the very high-resolution Pleiades satellites

status.value: In operation (SPOT-6, SPOT-7), terminated (SPOT-1 to SPOT-5)

base.reason: eoPortal mission page (ESA-run aggregator); page states 'The constellation has spatial resolution of 2m for panchromatic bands, and 8m for multispectral bands' (panchromatic GSD 1.5 m to 2.5 m at nadir); registry v2 EO spec backfill, cite-facts-only per terms
eoPortal mission page; page states a '60km swath width at nadir using two imagers, or 120km with a single pass mosaic'

revisit.value: daily revisit capability via the phased SPOT-6 & SPOT-7 constellation (1 day at 45 degrees to 5 days at 30 degrees incidence)

base.reason: eoPortal mission page; page states 'Daily revisit capability thanks to the phased constellation of SPOT-6 & SPOT-7' and a revisit 'capability 1 (45deg) to 5 days (30deg)'
eoPortal mission page; page states the NAOMI imager provides one panchromatic band, one Near infrared band (NIR), and three multispectral (blue, green, red) bands

notes: Seeded for the Orbits surface on Florian's instruction (2026-07-05); fields filled by the eoPortal EO spec backfill (2026-07-09) from the SPOT-6/7 mission page: NAOMI imager, 2 m panchromatic (1.5-2.5 m GSD at nadir) and 8 m multispectral, 60 km swath (120 km with both imagers), daily revisit via the phased two-satellite constellation, five bands (panchromatic, blue, green, red, NIR).

events.headline: SPOT 1 debuts CNES's commercial Earth observation program on Ariane 1
SPOT 1 ends operations after 17 years, deorbited in stages
SPOT 6 launches on PSLV, continuing Airbus's second-generation SPOT constellation
SPOT 7 launches on PSLV alongside SPOT 6 to form intraday-revisit pair
Airbus transfers SPOT 7 ownership to Azercosmos, renamed Azersky
